tools.name: authority_check_balance
tools.description:
Check a patron's credit balance at this operator.

This is the patron's spending balance — credits purchased via
Lightning for tool calls at this operator. For the operator's
own balance at the Authority (needed to certify patron purchases),
use authority_check_balance instead.

Free — no credits re

tools.name: authority_purchase_credits
tools.description:
Buy credits via Bitcoin Lightning.

Creates a Lightning invoice. Pay it with any Lightning wallet,
then call check_payment to confirm. Proof of npub ownership is
required so credits land in the correct ledger.

Free — no credits required to call.

Args:
    npub: The Nostr public key (npub1...) the

tools.name: authority_check_payment
tools.description:
Check the payment status of a Lightning invoice.

Call after paying the invoice from purchase_credits.
Free — no credits required. Proof of npub ownership is required
to prevent credit-grant front-running by an observer of the
invoice ID.

Args:
    invoice_id: The invoice ID returned by purchase_cr

tools.name: authority_restore_credits
tools.description:
Credit a patron's ledger from a BTCPay-settled invoice.

**RESTRICTED to the operator** — the operator owns the books and is
the only party who can issue a manual credit grant. Patrons who
believe they paid but never got credits must escalate to the
operator's support, who then invokes this tool on

tools.name: authority_account_statement
tools.description:
Generate a patron's account statement at this operator.

Returns the patron's purchase history, active credit tranches,
per-tool usage breakdown, and recent daily usage logs. This is
the patron's spending account — not the operator's Authority
tax balance.

Free — no credits consumed. Proof of npub

tools.name: authority_account_statement_infographic
tools.description:
Generate a visual SVG infographic of your account statement.

Returns the same data as account_statement, rendered as a dark-themed
SVG graphic with balance hero, metrics cards, health gauge, tranche
table, and tool usage breakdown. Costs 1 api_sat per call. Proof is
verified by ``debit_or_deny`` be

tools.name: authority_service_status
tools.description: Check the health and configuration of this service. Free.

tools.name: authority_get_operator_onboarding_status
tools.description:
Report this operator's configuration readiness.

Shows which operator settings are configured, which are missing,
and how to deliver each missing value. For patron-level credential
status, use get_patron_onboarding_status instead. Free.

tools.name: authority_get_patron_onboarding_status
tools.description:
Report a patron's credential readiness for this operator.

For set-once services (eXcalibur, TheBrain), shows which patron
secrets are configured and which are missing. For dynamic/OAuth2
services (Schwab), reports that no patron credentials are needed.
Free. Proof of npub ownership is required beca

tools.name: authority_session_status
tools.description:
Check operator readiness. Returns the operator lifecycle
state and clear guidance on what to do next. Free.

Lifecycle states:
- ready: Operator is warm and fully operational — vault AND pricing
  model verified. Proceed with tool calls.
- warming_up: Operator is initializing (cold start). Try a too

tools.name: authority_request_credential_channel
tools.description:
Open a Secure Courier channel for credential delivery.

This is the CREDENTIAL-DELIVERY flow — use it to hand over a service
secret (API keys, tokens). To merely prove you control an npub (the
usual answer to a ``proof_required`` error), use ``request_npub_proof``
instead. Note: dynamic/OAuth2 servi

tools.name: authority_receive_credentials
tools.description:
Pick up credentials from the Secure Courier.

Completes the CREDENTIAL-DELIVERY flow (the ownership-proof
counterpart is ``receive_npub_proof``).

**Call this only after the user confirms they have replied.**
Deterministic, one-shot retrieval: name the response you want with
``(sender_npub, service,

tools.name: authority_forget_credentials
tools.description:
Delete vaulted credentials for a specific service and npub.

For operator credentials, pass the operator's own npub. For patron
credentials, pass the patron's npub. Always requires proof of
npub ownership — a deletion is as destructive as a write.

Args:
    service: The credential service to forget

tools.name: authority_update_patron_credential
tools.description:
Add or update a single patron credential field.

Merges into existing stored credentials without affecting
other fields. Useful for setting an account identifier
after OAuth, changing a default brain, etc. Free. Proof of
npub ownership is required — this is a write to the patron's
sensitive credenti

tools.name: authority_delete_patron_credential
tools.description:
Remove a single patron credential field.

Deletes one field from stored credentials without affecting
other fields. Free. Proof of npub ownership is required —
this is a write to the patron's sensitive credential vault.

Args:
    npub: The patron's Nostr public key (npub1...).
    dpop_token: A kin

tools.name: authority_get_patron_credential_fields
tools.description:
List stored patron credential field names (not values).

Returns the names of fields stored for a patron. Values
are never exposed — use this to verify which fields are
configured. Free. Proof of npub ownership is required: the
list of configured fields is itself sensitive (reveals
which integration

tools.name: authority_request_npub_proof
tools.description:
Request npub ownership proof from a patron via Nostr DM.

This is the npub-OWNERSHIP-PROOF flow — use it when a call returns
``proof_required``. It proves the caller controls an npub; it does
NOT deliver any service secret. To hand an operator its API keys or
OAuth secrets, use ``request_credential_

tools.name: authority_receive_npub_proof
tools.description:
Receive npub ownership confirmation from a patron.

Completes the npub-OWNERSHIP-PROOF flow (the credential-delivery
counterpart is ``receive_credentials``).

**Call this only after the user confirms they have replied.**
Deterministic, one-shot retrieval: name the response with
``(patron_npub, dpop_

tools.name: authority_check_proof_status
tools.description:
Check whether a previously-cached dpop_token is still valid.

Mirrors ``check_oauth_status`` for the npub-proof flow: a calling
agent can ask "will my next paid call accept this dpop_token?"
before burning credits on a guaranteed failure.

Free, no side effects — does not evict the cache or touch re

tools.name: authority_oracle_how_to_join
tools.description: Get DPYC onboarding instructions from the Oracle. Free.

tools.name: authority_oracle_get_tax_rate
tools.description: Get the current DPYC certification tax rate. Free.

tools.name: authority_oracle_lookup_member
tools.description: Look up a DPYC community member by npub. Free.

tools.name: authority_oracle_about
tools.description: Describe the DPYC ecosystem via the Oracle. Free.

tools.name: authority_oracle_network_advisory
tools.description: Get active network advisories from the Oracle. Free.

tools.name: authority_check_authority_balance
tools.description:
Check this operator's tax balance at the Authority.

Returns the sats available for certifying patron credit purchases.
When this balance reaches zero, patron top-ups cannot be certified
and the operator must call purchase_credits on the Authority.

This is the operator's own funding — not a patron

tools.name: authority_get_pricing_model
tools.description:
Get the active pricing model for this operator. Free.

If no model exists, self-initializes a scaffold with all
registered tools at 0 sats.  No economic data from code.

tools.name: authority_set_pricing_model
tools.description:
Set the active pricing model. RESTRICTED to operator.

Requires a valid proof (Schnorr-signed kind-27235 event)
proving the caller holds the operator's nsec.

tools.name: authority_reset_pricing_model
tools.description:
Erase all pricing models and restore a viable default.

Deletes every stored model, then self-initializes a fresh one
from the tool registry — all tools at 0 sats with proper UUIDs.
Returns the new model.

RESTRICTED to operator — requires proof (nsec-signed).

tools.name: authority_request_adoption
tools.description:
Ask a chosen Authority to adopt this operator (deferred courtship).

RESTRICTED to the operator — requires proof the caller controls this
operator's npub. Resolves the Authority's MCP endpoint from the
community registry, mints an inline ownership proof with this
operator's nsec, and delivers the re

tools.name: authority_adoption_status
tools.description:
Check this operator's adoption-request status at a chosen Authority.

Free. Polls the Authority MCP-to-MCP for the status of this operator's
request (pending / approved / rejected / provisioned).

tools.name: authority_restore_neon_schema
tools.description:
Re-run ``ensure_schema()`` on every NeonVault this operator uses.

Diagnostic / recovery tool for the case where the Neon HTTP SQL API
is returning persistent 4xx errors and the operator suspects the
schema isn't there or grants are wrong. Idempotent — uses
``CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS`` so a succes

tools.name: authority_list_canonical_identities
tools.description:
Return canonical (tool_id, mcp_name, …) for every registered tool.

The authoritative source for any client (Studio, agents, FE)
that needs to know how this MCP identifies its tools.
Reconcile uses this output to UUID-join against the stored
pricing model — no name-based UUID derivation, no guessing

tools.name: authority_get_nostr_profile
tools.description:
Read an npub's public Nostr profile (NIP-01 kind-0 metadata).

Free, no proof — the data is already public on relays. Returns the
latest metadata fields (name, display_name, about, picture, banner,
nip05, website, lud16) or an empty profile if none is published.

tools.name: authority_publish_nostr_profile
tools.description:
Publish a CLIENT-SIGNED kind-0 profile to relays for an npub.

The wheel never holds a patron nsec. The frontend signs the kind-0
metadata event with the patron's session key or a NIP-07 extension and
passes the signed event (JSON) here; the wheel verifies the signature
matches the npub, then relays

tools.name: authority_check_price
tools.description:
Preview the effective cost of a tool call.

Shows the base cost and any constraint effects (discounts, free
trials, surge pricing). Free — no credits required.

Args:
    tool_id: Either the tool's UUID (from the pricing model) or a
        bare capability string (e.g. ``"deal_scenario"``). FE

tools.name: authority_list_constraint_types
tools.description:
List all available constraint types and their parameter schemas.

Returns the type, category, description, and parameter specs for
every constraint that can be used in a pricing pipeline.
Free — no credits required.

tools.name: authority_mint_coupon
tools.description:
Create a new operator-owned discount coupon.

Args:
    name: The catchy code patrons type to redeem
        (operator-scoped uniqueness).
    discount_percent: Percentage off the base price (0-100).
    valid_from: ISO-8601 datetime when the coupon becomes active.
    valid_until: ISO-8601 datetime

tools.name: authority_list_coupons
tools.description:
List every coupon this operator has minted (newest first).

Each row carries the current ``times_redeemed`` counter — the
Studio renders a progress bar from this against ``total_uses``.
RESTRICTED to operator — requires proof.

tools.name: authority_update_coupon
tools.description:
Patch a coupon's editable fields.

Pass only the fields you want to change.  To set a cap to
unlimited (NULL in the schema), pass ``clear_uses_per_patron=true``
or ``clear_total_uses=true``.  Renaming the code is allowed —
existing patron redemption rows survive (they key on coupon id).

RESTRICTED

tools.name: authority_delete_coupon
tools.description:
Delete a coupon.  Cascades to all patron redemptions.

Any chain step referencing the deleted coupon_id becomes a
no-op (the constraint returns neutral on unknown ids) — the
Studio surfaces orphan references as warnings.

RESTRICTED to operator — requires proof.

tools.name: authority_redeem_coupon
tools.description:
Claim a coupon by its name (the code the operator shared).

Looks up the operator's coupon by ``code``, validates the window
and total cap, and records a per-patron redemption row.
Subsequent paid tool calls on this MCP auto-apply the discount
until ``uses_per_patron`` is exhausted.

Free — no credi

tools.name: authority_list_my_coupons
tools.description:
List the coupons this patron has redeemed on this operator.

Returns both active and exhausted redemptions with a per-row
``status`` (``active`` / ``window_closed`` / ``patron_limit`` /
``total_limit``).  Free — requires proof of ``npub``.

tools.name: authority_forget_coupon
tools.description:
Remove a coupon from this patron's redemption list.

Cosmetic only — the coupon itself still exists at the operator,
and the patron can re-redeem the same code later while the
window allows.  Free — requires proof of ``npub``.

tools.name: authority_notarize_ledger
tools.description:
Build a Merkle tree of all patron balances and submit the root
to Bitcoin via OpenTimestamps.

Operator-only background task. Bitcoin confirmation takes 1-6 hours.
Free — no credits required.

tools.name: authority_get_notarization_proof
tools.description:
Generate a Merkle inclusion proof that a patron's balance was
included in a Bitcoin-notarized snapshot.

Args:
    notarization_id: The notarization record ID.
    npub: The patron's Nostr public key (npub1...).

tools.name: authority_list_notarizations
tools.description:
List recent Bitcoin notarization records.

Args:
    limit: Maximum records to return (default 20).
    status: Optional filter (e.g., 'submitted', 'confirmed').

tools.name: authority_register_operator
tools.description:
Provision an operator in the Authority ledger.

Creates a ledger entry so the operator can purchase credits and
certify purchase orders. Idempotent — safe to call again.

Requires TWO independent identity proofs:

1. ``proof`` — Schnorr proof signed by the candidate operator's
   ``npub``. Proves th

tools.name: authority_update_operator
tools.description:
Update an existing Operator's community registry entry.

Requires the same two proofs as ``register_operator``:

- ``proof`` proves the caller controls the Operator's ``npub``.
- ``authority_proof`` proves the Authority's human admin consents
  to the change. Without the Authority proof, anyone with

tools.name: authority_deregister_operator
tools.description:
Remove an Operator from the DPYC community registry.

Requires the same two proofs as ``register_operator``:

- ``proof`` proves the caller controls the Operator's ``npub``.
- ``authority_proof`` proves the Authority's human admin consents
  to the removal. Without the Authority proof, anyone who kn

tools.name: authority_get_operator_config
tools.description:
Retrieve operator bootstrap configuration (Neon URL, schema).

Gated by Schnorr signature proving ownership of the requested npub.

tools.name: authority_operator_status
tools.description:
View registration status, balance summary, and the Authority's Nostr npub.

When an explicit ``npub`` is provided, requires a Schnorr proof of
ownership — without it, anyone could enumerate balances by walking
the community registry. When ``npub`` is empty, falls back to the
Authority's own operator

tools.name: authority_certify_credits
tools.description:
Certify a purchase order: return a Schnorr-signed Nostr event certificate.

The paid_tool decorator handles the ad valorem fee debit and stores
the cost in runtime._last_debit_cost. No recomputation needed.

Called by operator MCP servers (not end users) when a patron purchases credits.

tools.name: authority_check_dpyc_membership
tools.description: Look up an npub in the DPYC community registry.

tools.name: authority_receive_adoption_request
tools.description:
Inbound: record an operator's request to be adopted by this Authority.

Called MCP-to-MCP by the operator's ``request_adoption``. Verifies the
operator controls ``operator_npub`` (inline Schnorr bound to the
adoption sentinel — no relay round-trip), records a durable ``pending``
row, and fires a bes

tools.name: authority_list_adoption_requests
tools.description:
Owner queue: list pending operator-adoption requests.

Restricted to the Authority owner (consent proof). This is the
review-on-your-own-time surface the Pricing Studio renders.

tools.name: authority_approve_adoption
tools.description:
Approve a pending request and provision the operator.

The deferred-courtship counterpart to register_operator: same
``authority_proof`` consent, same provisioning effect
(``_provision_operator``) — just supplied later, after review.

tools.name: authority_reject_adoption
tools.description: Reject a pending operator-adoption request (owner consent).

tools.name: authority_repair_operator_schema
tools.description:
Owner repair: reassign every table in an operator's tenant schema to
the operator's own role, then re-grant DML.

For tenants whose tables were created/owned by the provisioning role —
the operator role then cannot CREATE INDEX on them ("must be owner"),
which aborts the whole vault bootstrap. Unlik

tools.name: authority_get_adoption_status
tools.description:
Read an operator's adoption-request status (free, no proof).

Status (pending/approved/rejected/provisioned) isn't sensitive — it's
the operator's own request — so the operator can poll it openly via
its ``adoption_status`` tool.

tools.name: authority_register_authority_npub
tools.description: Step 1/3 of Authority onboarding — send a Nostr DM challenge to the candidate.

tools.name: authority_confirm_authority_claim
tools.description:
Step 2/3 of Authority onboarding — verify candidate DM, escalate to parent Authority.

The parent Authority is resolved from THIS Authority's own entry in
dpyc-community: whatever its ``upstream_authority_npub`` names. For
Lonnie-Authority and NorthAmerica that's Prime; for NewEngland it's
NorthAmer

tools.name: authority_check_authority_approval
tools.description: Step 3/3 of Authority onboarding — check parent approval, activate Authority.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "authority_get_patron_onboarding_status" references sensitive artifacts (credentials, secret). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Report a patron's credential readiness for this operator. For set-once services (eXcalibur, TheBrain), shows which patron secrets are configured and which are m…

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "authority_request_credential_channel" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Open a Secure Courier channel for credential delivery. This is the CREDENTIAL-DELIVERY flow — use it to hand over a service secret (API keys, tokens). To merely…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: authority_session_status, authority_request_credential_channel, authority_receive_credentials, authority_request_npub_proof, authority_receive_npub_proof, authority_get_operator_config, authority_receive_adoption_request, authority_list_adoption_requests · Private data: authority_purchase_credits, authority_restore_credits, authority_get_operator_onboarding_status, authority_get_patron_onboarding_status, authority_session_status, authority_request_credential_channel, authority_receive_credentials, authority_forget_credentials, authority_update_patron_credential, authority_delete_patron_credential, authority_get_patron_credential_fields, authority_request_npub_proof, authority_receive_npub_proof, authority_request_adoption, authority_publish_nostr_profile, authority_mint_coupon, authority_redeem_coupon, authority_get_notarization_proof, authority_list_notarizations, authority_receive_adoption_request, authority_repair_operator_schema · Public sink: authority_request_credential_channel, authority_publish_nostr_profile, authority_notarize_ledger, authority_repair_operator_schema, authority_register_authority_npub · Destructive: authority_forget_credentials, authority_delete_patron_credential, authority_reset_pricing_model, authority_restore_neon_schema, authority_delete_coupon, authority_forget_coupon, authority_deregister_operator.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: authority_session_status, authority_request_credential_channel, authority_receive_credentials, authority_request_npub_proof, authority_receive_npub_proof, authority_get_operator_config, authority_receive_adoption_request, authority_list_adoption_requests · Private data: authority_purchase_credits, authority_restore_credits, authority_get_operator_onboarding_status, authority_get_patron_onboarding_status, authority_session_status, authority_request_credential_channel, authority_receive_credentials, authority_forget_credentials, authority_update_patron_credential, authority_delete_patron_credential, authority_get_patron_credential_fields, authority_request_npub_proof, authority_receive_npub_proof, authority_request_adoption, authority_publish_nostr_profile, authority_mint_coupon, authority_redeem_coupon, authority_get_notarization_proof, authority_list_notarizations, authority_receive_adoption_request, authority_repair_operator_schema · Public sink: authority_request_credential_channel, authority_publish_nostr_profile, authority_notarize_ledger, authority_repair_operator_schema, authority_register_authority_npub · Destructive: authority_forget_credentials, authority_delete_patron_credential, authority_reset_pricing_model, authority_restore_neon_schema, authority_delete_coupon, authority_forget_coupon, authority_deregister_operator

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
